extraction.one_line_summary: POGAČAR attacked solo on Col de la Colombière, gained over 5 minutes on GC contenders, took yellow jersey.
tactical_patterns: POGAČAR attacked on Colombière without team support, revealing UAE weakness can be offset by overwhelming individual strength in mountains.
CARAPAZ blew up after riding well on Col de Romme, showing poor pacing judgment between consecutive Cat 1 climbs.
URÁN did zero work in groups all stage, conserving energy for later Tour podium attempts despite positioning opportunities.
POELS attacked uncategorized opening climb for KOM points, demonstrating breakaway riders must commit from kilometer zero on mountain stages.

rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: Gained 5+ minutes on all GC rivals with solo attack on Colombière, exposing gap to field despite weak team.
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: negative: Blew up badly after strong start on Romme, suggesting pacing issues on consecutive Cat 1 climbs.
rider_signals.URÁN Rigoberto: positive: Moved into top 10 doing minimal work, smart energy conservation suggests podium form building.
rider_signals.POELS Wout: positive: Attacked from km 0 on uncategorized climb, took KOM lead through relentless early aggression.
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: Moved to second overall, limiting losses on mountain stage beyond typical classics rider capability.
rider_signals.LUTSENKO Alexey: positive: Third overall suggests time trial strength translating to mountain resistance better than expected.
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: Five minutes ahead in white jersey competition at age 22, showing future Tour contention threat.
rider_signals.WOODS Michael: neutral: Strong KOM showing suggests targeting polka dots with climbing kick, not overall GC.
